## Summary

<!-- Describe what this pull request changes and why. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] `feat`: New feature
- [ ] `fix`: Bug fix
- [ ] `docs`: Documentation-only change
- [ ] `test`: Test-only change
- [ ] `refactor`: Refactor without behavior change
- [ ] `build` / `ci`: Build, release, or CI change
- [ ] `chore`: Maintenance change

## Related issues

<!-- Link related issues, discussions, or release notes. Use "Closes #123" when applicable. -->

## Implementation notes

<!-- Explain important design decisions, trade-offs, or migration notes. -->

## Testing

<!-- Describe what you ran locally and include relevant output when useful. -->

- [ ] Added or updated tests where appropriate.
- [ ] Ran targeted tests for the changed package or command.
- [ ] Ran the full test suite, or explained why it was not necessary.

Commands run:

```sh
# pnpm format:check
# pnpm build
# pnpm typecheck
# pnpm lint
# pnpm test
```

## Documentation

<!-- Documentation is part of done for user-facing changes. -->

- [ ] Updated the relevant docs page under `apps/docs/content/docs/`.
- [ ] Updated shared README fragments under `docs/shared/` when README content changed.
- [ ] Ran `pnpm format` when README fragments or generated README blocks changed.
- [ ] Not applicable because this is not user-facing.

## Release readiness

- [ ] Added a changeset for published package changes.
- [ ] Did not manually edit package versions.
- [ ] Confirmed relative TypeScript imports use `.js` extensions.
- [ ] Confirmed docs and user-facing CLI output do not introduce emojis.

## Security checklist

- [ ] No secrets, tokens, credentials, or private data are included.
- [ ] Dependencies were added only when necessary and use the workspace catalog where applicable.
- [ ] Security-sensitive changes are documented in the implementation notes.
- [ ] Expected GitGuardian and Snyk checks are clean, or findings are explained.

## Screenshots or output

<!-- Add screenshots, terminal output, or before/after examples when helpful. -->

## Reviewer notes

<!-- Anything reviewers should focus on first? -->
